Add a Reaction to an ODE

reaction-set.Rd

This function operates on an ODE vector field. It makes it possible to construct the right hand side one reaction at a time: reaction(vf,r,p) <- flux will add a flux term to the ODE's right hand side (vector field), adding the given flux (reaction kinetic) in the right places, informed by the reactants and products.

Usage

reaction(vf, r, p) <- value

Arguments

vf

a named character vector of the right length (number of state variables)

r

a named vector of stoichiometric coefficients for the reactants

p

a named vector of stoichiometric coefficients for the products

value

a reaction rate (string)

Value

updated vf

Details

The reactants, e.g.: c(A=2,B=1) name which state variables are affected by the flux (negatively, for reactants) The products, e.g.: c(C=1) name which state variables are affected positively by the flux.

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
Reaction <- "A + B <=> C"
r <- c(A=1,B=1)
p <- c(C=1)
vf <- c(A="",B="",C="") # empty
reaction(vf,r,p) <- "A*B-C"
print(vf)
} # }
